title: Memory locations should not be released more than once
description: Using delete or free releases the reservation on a memory location, making it immediately available for another purpose. Releasing the exact memory location twice leads to undefined behavior and can often crash the program. The C standard defines as undefined behavior a call to free with a pointer to a memory area that has already been released.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Memory locations should not be released more than once. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
